author: weeknight_kitchen
caption: 15-minute garlic butter shrimp pasta 🍝
transcript: Start by boiling your linguine in salted water. Melt butter with garlic, add shrimp, deglaze with white wine, toss with pasta and parsley.
ocr_text:
GARLIC BUTTER SHRIMP PASTA
serves 2
30 min
title: Garlic Butter Shrimp Pasta
summary: A quick weeknight linguine with garlic butter shrimp, finished with white wine and parsley.
key_points: Boil 8 oz linguine in salted water
Melt 4 tbsp butter with 4 cloves minced garlic
Add 1 lb shrimp, cook 2 min per side
Toss with pasta, 1/4 cup parsley, lemon

steps: Boil linguine in salted water until al dente
Add shrimp, cook 2 min per side
Deglaze with white wine, reduce 1 min
Toss with pasta, parsley, lemon juice

author: buildinpublic
caption: I wired an LLM into my note-taking with a tiny local agent.
transcript: The trick is you keep the agent stateless and let a queue file be the source of truth.
title: Stateless local note agent
summary: A pattern for a small local LLM agent that ingests notes, using a queue file as the single source of truth.
key_points: Use a queue.jsonl as source of truth
assessment: This overlaps with the queue-as-source-of-truth idea you already use for capture storage. The stateless-agent framing is worth borrowing for any new worker: it makes retries safe. Verdict: experiment — low cost to try the queue pattern on one new flow.

author: study_smarter
caption: The Feynman technique in 60 seconds.
transcript: Explain the concept in plain language as if teaching a child, find the gaps, then refine.
ocr_text:
FEYNMAN TECHNIQUE
1 teach it simply
2 find gaps
3 refine
title: Feynman technique
summary: A quick explainer of the Feynman learning technique: teach simply, find gaps, refine.
key_points: Explain the concept in plain language
Identify where the explanation breaks down
Go back to the source to fill gaps
